title: Longest Increasing Path in Matrix
description:
Given an `m x n` integer matrix, return the length of the longest increasing path.

From each cell, you can move in four directions: left, right, up, or down. You **may not** move diagonally or outside the boundary. A path must be **strictly increasing**.

Example:
```
[[9,9,4],
 [6,6,8],
 [2,1,1]]
```
Longest path: `1 → 2 → 6 → 9`, length `4`.
hints: DFS from every cell, memoize results to avoid recomputation.
A cell's result only depends on strictly larger neighbors — no cycles possible.
The memo table stores 'longest path starting from cell (i,j)'.
